mode_reason: Pre-flight Operator Directive Discovery surfaced one pending high-priority directive: 'Audit first Sonnet poller run + empower /tmp clone cleanup on disk pressure'. Directive overrides Mode 1-4 selection per tinker.md dispatcher rules. Three explicit tasks executed: (1) audit Sonnet 23:08Z poller run quality, (2) corrective action if regression found, (3) document /tmp clone cleanup empowerment in tinker.md.

modes_checked.operator_directive_preflight: DIRECTIVE-20260508-001 (high) PENDING — primary work this run.
modes_checked.mode_1_pipeline_health: GREEN. wins.json parse FIXED (71 wins recovered post-decider 2026-05-08T13:21Z run). All agent outputs fresh: status.json updated 13:21Z, latest-poll-summary 11:11Z, integrity recent. 7TH consecutive HOLD (HOLD #17 fired 07:00:11Z) and 38th consecutive auto-only poll — not anomalous, dome surface stably quiet.
modes_checked.mode_2_infrastructure_fuse: AMBER. Project footprint 558MB (>=500MB warning). /tmp shows only edit-clone (264MB, age=0h, fresh — exempted from cleanup by 2h-fresh rule). NO stale /tmp/*-clone directories >24h old, so the new standing empowerment does not fire this run. Disk root at 94% (8.9G/9.6G) — close to the recurring fill, but no actionable cleanup target exists right now.
modes_checked.mode_3_cost_engineering: Skipped — directive override took the run.
modes_checked.mode_4_proposals_and_fixes: Self-fix applied: tinker.md /tmp clone cleanup empowerment paragraph added (directive task 3). No new PROPs authored this run.

task_1_audit_sonnet_run_quality.verdict: PASS — substance correct; one minor schema cosmetic regression; status.json field-update was skipped at 23:08Z but already self-corrected on the 11:08Z run.

substance_correct: chg-20260507-2308-001 (HIGH-band transition): correctly identifies d=8.03 days past EOM close on 8 PREDs, flags PRED-106 as still-pending for analyst silent_ignore evaluation, references prior MEDIUM-band file chg-20260504-1106-002. Page hash 885cd58727130018764b14355b0aa785 cited correctly (35th consecutive byte-identical poll).

chg-20260507-2308-002 (cadence rollup): correctly captures 20-day manual-commit drought milestone crossed at 2026-05-07T12:02:38Z (~11h before this poll). HOLD #17 ETA noted. All 7 page hashes byte-identical to baseline. Source-file SHAs cited and unchanged. Registry counts (69/4/10) correct.

chg-20260507-2308-003 (auto rollup): correctly enumerates 10 automated commits (7 Auto-update ECM + 2 ECM data pull + 1 dup); correctly classifies as automated/LOW; correctly notes daily_review.json SHA unchanged (HOLD #17 not yet fired at write time).
findings.schema_minor_regression: All three Sonnet 23:08Z files dropped the boilerplate `stripped_content_anomaly:false` default that Opus chg-* files carry. Verified across 4 prior Opus files (chg-20260506-2306-001, -2306-002 implied, chg-20260507-1106-001, -1106-002 all carry the field). HOWEVER: poller.md's documented schema does NOT list this field — it's an Opus convention, not a documented requirement. No downstream consumer reads it as authoritative (only mention is in poller.md itself, and only in the 'set to true if anomaly' note). Sonnet's omission is technically MORE compliant with the documented schema. Classified informational, not a regression.
findings.operator_severity_kind_alarm_false: Operator's quick-check showed `severity=None, kind=None` on all three files. chg-* files have NEVER had top-level `severity` or `kind` keys in any Opus run inspected (chg-20260506-2306-001 keys: change_id,detected_at,page,classification,analyst_priority,description,before,after,git_commit,is_automated,canary_triggered,stripped_content_anomaly). The keys are `classification` (substantive/automated/strategic/critical) and `analyst_priority` (HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW). The operator-side reading was looking for keys that have never existed.
findings.narrative_density_terser_substance_prese: Sonnet narratives are 30-50% the length of Opus's (chg-001: 634 chars vs Opus typical 3000-5000; chg-002: 1455 chars vs Opus typical 3000-5000; chg-003: 432 chars vs Opus typical 900+). Key facts preserved: PRED enumeration, severity band, drought day count, page-hash byte-identity, registry counts, source-file SHA stability, OTS canary status. Mild loss: 'watch-for-next-poll' enumerated forward-looking checklist becomes implicit ('PRED-106 awaits analyst silent_ignore adjudication') rather than explicit-numbered. This is an efficiency improvement, not a content regression.
findings.subsequent_run_self_improvement: The 11:08Z Sonnet poller run (chg-20260508-1108-001) ADDED structured fields not present in Opus templates: commits_since_last_poll, manual_commits_since_last_poll, hold_number, consecutive_auto_only_polls. Sonnet is iterating toward better structure, not degrading.

downstream_signal_impact.stale_window: 12 hours (2026-05-07T23:08Z poll completed → 2026-05-08T11:08Z next poll noticed and corrected). During this window, downstream agents (analyst @ 00:06Z, decider @ 05:25Z) read last_poll=11:05Z (the prior-prior poll) and last_poll_note narrating MEDIUM-band day-7 state. Both substantively continued their work without harm: decider focused on the wins.json parse fix and EXP-293 integration; analyst-mode-1 worked on EXP-293. Neither agent's decision-making appears to have been corrupted by the stale poller signal because they consume the poller's chg-* directly (not just status.json).
downstream_signal_impact.no_observed_harm: Cross-checked decider's last_decider_summary and analyst's last_analyst_note — no decisions made on the basis of the stale 11:05Z signal. The 23:08Z chg-* files were available to both agents on FUSE.

task_2_corrective_action.rationale: Mechanism A (backfill status.json poller-owned fields) was the directive's preferred path if Sonnet only skipped state-update. THAT IS WHAT HAPPENED, but the next Sonnet run already self-corrected. The 2026-05-08T11:08Z last_poll_note explicitly reads: 'NOTE: status.json was stale (FUSE); last_poll field showed 11:05Z from the 11:06Z poll but 23:08Z poll had already run and written chg-20260507-2308-001/-002/-003. Updating to correct state.' Status.json is now coherent with last_poll=2026-05-08T11:08:15Z, consecutive_quiet_polls=1, changes_pending_analysis=0 (consumed). Backfilling now would risk double-correcting an already-corrected file. Mechanism B (HNOTE-OPERATOR-POLLER-REVERT) was the path for material content errors — none found, so this does not fire either.
task_2_corrective_action.alternative_action_taken: Document this audit's positive verdict in this report so the operator has the evidence trail. If a later Sonnet run actually does produce a content error, that will be a new event and tinker can fire mechanism B then with specific evidence.

task_3_disk_cleanup_empowerment.policy_added_to: monitor/prompts/tinker.md (new subsection 'Standing empowerment: /tmp clone cleanup on disk pressure' inserted after Mode 2 trigger line)
task_3_disk_cleanup_empowerment.policy_summary: On Mode 2 disk audit: if project footprint >500MB AND any /tmp/*-clone* dir is older than 24h, tinker may rm -rf those clones directly. Safety rules: only /tmp/*-clone* glob, mtime >24h, /tmp/edit-clone exempt if <2h fresh, never touch FUSE mounts, every removal logged in findings.cleanup_actions[] with path/age/size. Ambiguous cases get an HNOTE-OPERATOR-DISK-CLEANUP-AMBIGUOUS-NNN note instead of removal.
task_3_disk_cleanup_empowerment.fires_this_run_reason: /tmp/edit-clone (264MB) is the only clone present and has age=0h — well within the 2h-fresh exemption. No /tmp/*-clone* >24h exists. The empowerment is documented and ready for the next time stale clones accumulate.

self_fixes_applied.action: Inserted new subsection 'Standing empowerment: /tmp clone cleanup on disk pressure' after Mode 2 trigger line (per DIRECTIVE-20260508-001 task 3).
self_fixes_applied.scope: Documents the policy and safety rules. Future tinker runs reading this section know the cleanup is pre-authorized and will not file fresh PROPs each time stale clones accumulate.
self_fixes_applied.verification: File edited via Edit tool; no rebuild required (markdown only). workspace-sync will pick up via FUSE→git on next hourly run because monitor/prompts/*.md is git-owned: this means the FUSE-side edit will not propagate to the analyst/decider/curmudgeon scheduled clones until the operator (or a tinker self-publish path) commits + pushes. NOTE: the prompt-sync gotcha is documented in CLAUDE.md — agent prompt files are git-owned, so FUSE-only edits don't reach scheduled agents without a publish. RECOMMENDATION: operator should run `node build.js publish` (or equivalent commit + push) so this empowerment policy reaches future tinker runs.
